30.reason: [Calling DFS with an invalid vertex index (109) which is out of range (vertices are 0 to n-1). This causes undefined behavior and incorrect traversal. Should call DFS before deletion without marking any irrelevant vertex as visited.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Calling DFS with an invalid vertex index (109) which is out of range (vertices are 0 to n-1). This causes undefined behavior and incorrect traversal. Should call DFS before deletion without marking any irrelevant vertex as visited.]

31.reason: [Same issue as above: passing 109 to BFS marks an out-of-bound vertex as visited, corrupting the traversal logic.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Same issue as above: passing 109 to BFS marks an out-of-bound vertex as visited, corrupting the traversal logic.]

32.reason: [After deletion, the graph structure hasn't been modified (edges to 'del' still exist), so DFS(del) incorrectly marks 'del' as visited but doesn't remove its edges, leading to wrong traversal results. Also, should not pass 'del' as argument to DFS/BFS functions since traversal always starts at 0.]

47.reason: [Marks an arbitrary or invalid 'point' (like 109 or 'del') as visited before starting traversal from 0, which may block access to valid nodes or cause undefined behavior if point >= MAXSIZE.]
